topic: Mistral Small 4 Review 2026: EU-Compliant, Open-Weight, $0.40/M Input

key_points: Describes Mistral Small 4 as the first MoE model to unify reasoning, vision, and coding in one
Highlights Apache 2.0 license as key differentiator vs proprietary models
Covers 119B total / 6.5B active parameters via 128 experts (4 active per token)
Rating: 8.4/10 as the strongest open-weight small model for combined tasks
Frames review as 'One Model, Three Jobs' — replacing Magistral, Pixtral, Devstral
Notes 40% latency reduction and 3x throughput vs Mistral Small 3
Emphasizes EU data residency and GDPR compliance for European enterprises
Highlights reasoning_effort parameter for tunable inference cost vs quality
Explains self-hosting requirements: 4x NVIDIA HGX H100, 2x HGX H200, or 1x DGX B200
Weights are 242GB in BF16 precision on HuggingFace
Apache 2.0 allows commercial products without royalties or usage restrictions
Native image input supports document analysis, chart reading, visual QA
AA Intelligence Index score of 28 (composite benchmark)
On AA LCR: scores 0.72 with only 1.6K characters vs Qwen needing 3.5-4x more output
Outperforms GPT-OSS 120B on LiveCodeBench while producing 20% shorter outputs
Price analysis: $0.15/M input, $0.60/M output via Mistral API
VentureBeat: 'consolidates reasoning, vision and coding at a fraction of the inference cost'
Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) improves inference efficiency without quality loss
Model ID on API: mistral-small-2603, also available via OpenRouter
Released March 16/17, 2026 with almost no prior announcement

stats.fact: Mistral Small 4 has 119 billion total parameters with only ~6.5 billion active per token (uses 4 of 128 experts per forward pass)
Mistral Small 4 pricing: $0.15 per 1M input tokens and $0.60 per 1M output tokens via Mistral API
Claude Haiku 4.5 costs $1.00/M input vs Mistral Small 4 at $0.15/M input — Mistral is 6.7x cheaper on input tokens
Mistral Small 4 delivers 40% reduction in end-to-end completion time and 3x more requests per second vs Mistral Small 3
Context window: 256K tokens (vs Claude Haiku 4.5 at 200K and GPT-4o mini's standard window)
Self-hosting requires minimum: 4x NVIDIA HGX H100 GPUs, 2x HGX H200, or 1x DGX B200; full weights are 242GB in BF16
HumanEval coding benchmark: 92% — comparable to Claude Haiku 3.5 and Qwen 2.5
On AA LCR, Mistral Small 4 scores 0.72 with only 1.6K output characters; Qwen needs 5.8–6.1K for comparable performance
Released March 16, 2026 under Apache 2.0 license — allows commercial use, self-hosting, fine-tuning without restrictions
As a French company, Mistral AI processes all API data within the EU by default — fully GDPR-compliant with no transatlantic data transfers

angles: The EU sovereignty angle: first serious open-weight model designed with GDPR-compliant EU data residency by default — critical for European enterprises that can't use US-based API providers

One model replaces three: Mistral merged Magistral (reasoning) + Pixtral (vision) + Devstral (coding) into a single system with a reasoning_effort dial — simplifies deployment and reduces ops overhead
The open-weight cost arbitrage: at $0.15/M input, it's 6.7x cheaper than Claude Haiku 4.5 while matching it on coding benchmarks — making it a strong budget replacement for production workflows
Apache 2.0 as moat: in a world where OpenAI/Anthropic/Google keep weights locked, Mistral bets that open > closed — enables fine-tuning, on-prem deployment, and IP ownership
MoE efficiency unlocked: 119B total params but only 6.5B active per token — delivers large-model quality at small-model inference cost, enabled by 4-of-128 expert routing
Hardware reality check: self-hosting requires 4x H100 minimum — Apache 2.0 is free but the GPU bill is not, making the API tier the realistic option for most teams
outline: H2: What Is Mistral Small 4? (119B MoE That Replaced Three Models)
H2: Key Specs at a Glance (Architecture, Context, Pricing, License)
H2: EU Compliance and GDPR: Why It Matters for European Teams
H2: Performance Benchmarks (HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, AA LCR vs Competitors)
H2: Mistral Small 4 vs Claude Haiku 4.5 vs GPT-4o Mini vs Qwen 2.5
H2: Use Cases: When Mistral Small 4 Is the Right Choice
H2: API Access vs Self-Hosting: Hardware Requirements and Real Costs
H2: Final Verdict: Who Should Use Mistral Small 4 in 2026?
